jobRequirements:
Senior Draughtsman is mainly posted either under Chief Draftsman or independently in Division offices headed by Executive Engineers. They assist Chief Draftsman or Executive Engineers relating to the matters of procurement of works & services through tendering process. They are the record keepers of technical files and records of their respective offices. The works of this post are of highly responsible, sensitive, confidential and time bound in nature as the works are mainly related to procurement of works or services through tendering process.
They have to perform or to assist their higher officer performing following works of Drawing Branches of Circle Offices / Division offices:
1. Preparation of drawings as and when required. Assist JES/AEs of Sub-divisions preparing estimates for works/services.
2. Checking of estimates, revised estimates of Division level and processing for sanctioning of the competent authority followed by keeping records.
3. Being the head of section (wherever applicable). receiving of dak and file marking and filing the letters correspondences.
4. Checking of draft tenders, processing and submission to higher authorities for approval.
5. Preparation of tender documents specification, terms & conditions of work of different tenders/works
6. Processing of tenders through Central Public Procurement Portal (e-tendering) / Government e-Marketplace (GeM).
7. Evaluation of technical / financial bids for tenders relating to procurement of works & services.
8. Preparation of minutes of meetings of Tender Committee of FBP & other committees on various matters related to works / supplies etc.
9. Overall monitoring of ongoing works from commencement to completion. All correspondences during execution of works and after completion of the works.
10. Maintain and processing fortnightly / monthly quarterly progress reports to the competent authorities.
11. Checking of variation statement, supplementary item statement and substitute item statement of work and processing for approval of competent authorities.
12. Preparation of survey report, write-off cases and all related correspondences to auction sale of unserviceable materials / equipment of department.
13. Record keeping of various registers, correspondences related to procurement of works and services and providing information as and when required like court cases / audit paras, RTI queries, etc.
14. Assisting higher authorities in specially assigned occasional duties like arrangement of TAC meetings, visit of Ministers, VIPs and other senior dignitaries and officers from Ministry and other departments etc.
15. Any other duties as assigned by higher authorities as and when require.
qualificationDescriptions:
Diploma in Engineering (Civil or Mechanical or Electrical) from a recognized University or Institute.
Experience:
Two years' experience in the field of Civil or Electrical or Mechanical Engineering from Government (Central or State or UTs) or statutory or autonomous Organisation or PSUs or Universities or Recognised Research Institutes.

GENERAL: When the Stores Superintendent/ Stores Keeper (Technical)/ Store Keeper goes on regular leave or is transferred. He should submit a charge report (Form No. 37) to his successor. The report should be in triplicate; the original with the Gazetted Officer's remarks, if any, thereof should be the office copy, the duplicate and the triplicate will be personnel copies of the incoming and the outgoing Stores Supdt./ Store Keeper (Technical)/ Store Keeper respectively. Will perform such other duties in his/her respective functional sphere as may be assigned to him/her from time to time by his/her superiors/ controlling Officer

jobRequirements:
i.	Processing of fish, prawns and lobsters have to be arranged and personally supervised according to the

          supervise also.

          ice in addition to all preliminary requisition for the conduct of the processing work.

          with ISI standards, hygienic handling of work by peelers and general sanitary condition of the processing hall.

          freezing after checking the quality and recording exact weight.

          and export have to be attended.
vii.	The PQAS are jointly responsible for the chill room freezer store and freezers and for the safe custody of its 
          keys.
viii.	Maintenance of processing registers and articles, daily submission of processing abstract have to be attended 
          to.
ix.	R&D work for product development.
x.	Implementation of HACCP.
xi.	Participation in extension activities.
xii.	Participation in training programmes in theory and practicals.

jobRequirements:
1. Ward Attendants are deployed to be vigilant around the clock over the Mental patients of the asylum. 
2. Ward Attendants play a very crucial role in providing personal care and Nursing of mental patients. Mentally ill patients are admitted in CIP without guardians, so these Ward Attendants are responsible to carefully and attentively look after the absconding, suicidal and homicidal Mental patients. 
3. Ward Attendants takes hand over/charge the admitted patients to the other Ward Attendants and mentioned and sign in-charge book in every shift duty. 
4. Ward Attendants are responsible that Mental patients are kept clean and properly clothed and patient receive proper attention from the Barber. 
5. Ward Attendants are also responsible to give Meal (breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) to Mentally ill patients who is admitted indoor the CIP Hospital. 
6. Ward Attendants are responsible to take patients for all types of Tests like ECT, EEG, ECG, CT scan, MRI and Blood Test of the admitted patients recommended by the Doctors. 
7. Ward Attendants are also responsible for admission and discharge process formalities for mentally ill patients recommended by doctors. 
8. Ward Attendants are also responsible to take physically ill patients to RIMS/other referral hospitals for tests and treatment along with the patients who are refer by Medical Officers.
qualificationDescriptions:

2. First Aid Certificate from an Institution recognised by the Central Governement or a State Government; and

jobRequirements:
a)	The Panchkarma Assistant will be responsible to conduct prescribed Panchkarma Therapies by Medical Officer cum Supdt. Physicians/Physicians as and when required. 
b)	The Panchkarma Therapist will at all times be courteous and helpful in dealing with the patient and under no circumstance enter into arguments whatsoever with a beneficiary, but  report the matter to the Medical Officer/Supdt./Sr. Physician/Physician on duty.
c)	She/He will be in her/his seat at the Panchkarma Section 15 minutes before the therapy to ensure cleanliness of the Section, to get prepare the decoctions and arrange the oils and required utensils etc. For the prescribed therapy. 
d)	He/She will be personally responsible to see that Pnachkarma room, utensils, tables etc. Are neat and clean and specially take care of it and ensure that after each patient therapy, tables and utensils are cleaned so that no patient carries any infection from one to another. 
e)	Panchkarma Therapist will maintain the records of expenditure and prepare the demand indents and will be responsible to keep the records of used oils and raw materials. 
f)	The Panchkarma Therapists will wear white overcoat type aprons while on duty. 
g)	The Panchkarma Therapist will also perform duties on holidays/Sundays as per duty roster. 
h)	The Panchkarma Therapist will prepare daily summary of given therapies to individuals patients and obtain counter signatures of Medical Supdt./ Sr. Physician/ Physician on duty after getting it verified from Sister on duty.
i)	The Panchkarma Therapist will perform such other duties as may be assigned to him/her by the Directorate/Medical Supdt. Commensurating with the nature of work.
qualificationDescriptions:
1.	12th Pass from a recognized Board/University.
2.	Diploma in Panchkarma treatment from a recognized Board or University

jobRequirements:
1.	The Pharmacist will be personally responsible for the correct dispensing as per prescriptions issued by him. He will issue medicines, mixtures, etc. In accordance with the instructions issued by the Directorate/Medical Stores/Depot/Medical Officer Incharge from time to time. 
2.	The pharmacist will maintain proper account of the items issued to him, dispensed by him and the balance in stock with him

5.	The pharmacist will maintain proper account of the items issued to him, dispensed by him and the balance in stock with him.

7.	She/he will perform any other duty assigned by Medical Officer Incharge commensurate with the nature of work.
qualificationDescriptions:
1. 12th Class passed or equivalent with Physics, Chemistry or Biology from a recognized Board or university;
2. Diploma or certificate course in Homeopathic Pharmacy of at least one year duration from a recognized institution.

Stores related job in GSI requires the receipt issue, safe custody and security of stores and equipment - under the charge of stores keepers, ensure that items of stores are not permited to be accumulated large in excess of the requirement as approved by the dept., proper labelling and pinning of store items, checking of physical balance of items under charge, proper stacking and issue of such items under charge alongwith voucher duly approved by stores suptd./stores officer and any other duties pertaining to receipt, issue maintenance, accounting and safe custody of the Stores, as deemed necessary by the officer — in Charge of the respective Stores Section.

jobRequirements:
"DUTIES OF SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT AT CENTRAL INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT CENTRE
Field Duties
To assist in conducting surveys to monitor insect pests, diseases, weeds bio-control agents situation of various crops in the fields.
To assist in popularizing Integrated Pest Management techniques at farmers’ field for the benefit of farmers.
To assist in conserving and augmenting natural enemies for the biological control of crop pests, diseases and weeds.
To assist in training programmes in IPM including biocontrol to state officers and farmers.
To assist in collection and maintaining information about insect pests, diseases and weeds situation including the methods of their control and plant protection equipments.
To assist in organization and development of all kinds of plant protection work and to perform other duties in accordance with the official order of the superiors or controlling officers.

LABORATORY DUTIES
To assist in mass rearing of host insects, parasitoids, predators, insect pathogens and phytophagous insects under laboratory conditions on natural and artificial diets.
To assist in collection, pinning, setting and labeling of insect and herbarium specimens. 
DUTIES OF SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT AT PLANT QUARANTINE STATIONS
To carry out and assist in the inspection of plants and plant material meant for export and also the consignments of plant and plant materials received as import.
To assist in treatment supervision and treatment of plants and plant materials imported from abroad if such treatment becomes necessary on examination of the import/export consignments.
To look after the proper upkeep and maintenance of fumigation equipment viz., fumigation chamber, electric blowers, applications etc.
DUTIES OF SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT AT LOCUST-CUM-INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT CENTRE(ERSTWHILE KNOWN AS LOCUST WARNING OFFICES)
To conduct Desert Locust Survey in Scheduled Desert Areas (SDA) and beyond, if required.
To assist in prevention/ control of upsurge/invasion of Desert Locust in India through prompt control operations.
To assist in Indo-Pak Border meetings for exchange of locust situation information between two Countries as and when arranged.
To associate in training organized for the Farmers, State functionaries and locust staff on latest locust control technologies.
To advise state functionaries, BSF personnel, Panchayati Raj Institutions during survey to inform the nearest LCIPMC(erstwhile LWO office), if any locust activity was reported in their areas for further needful action.
To assist senior officers in arranging International/National meetings/conferences.
To assist seniors in preparing the various technical as well as financial reports.
To assist seniors in performing any other day to day activities assigned from time to time.
DUTIES AT FIELD STATION INVESTIGATION ON LOCUST 
To assist in conduct of research on bio-efficacy of pesticides and bio-pesticides for locust control.
To conduct locust and grasshoppers survey in Scheduled Desert Areas (SDA) and beyond, if required.
To assist in collecting and identification of flora of locusts in Scheduled Desert Areas (SDA).
To assist in study of biotic factors of locusts and grasshoppers in the laboratory.
To assist in handling of scientific instruments in the laboratory.
To assist the seniors in preparing the various technical as well as financial reports.
To assist seniors in performing any other day to day activities assigned from time to time.        

To perform duties at RPTL/CIL/CIB&RC.
"
qualificationDescriptions: B.Sc with Botany or Zoology or B.Sc in Agriculture from a recognized university.

jobRequirements:
1. To assist the Research Officer in supervision of various activities pertaining to preparation, testing and quality control of biological products and other laboratory works.
2. Preparation of SOPs, Data log book, Data recording forms and implementations of the same and to assist in strict compliance of SOPs.
3. To prepare specifications for the procurement of raw material and equipment.
4. Compilation of Lab animal requirement, indents and approvals from CPCSE/IAEC.
5. Issuance of Lab Animals for experimental use as per approved protocols and record maintenance.
6. Revision of SOPs & other documentation work assigned by HOD.
7. Documentation and preparation for validation and calibration activities.
8. Supervision of various work in connection with production and testing of immunobiological products.
9. To perform IPQC/ Bulk and final Lot testing of immunobiologicals.
10. To perform equine immunization, batch processing, formulation and containerization of therapeutic antisera.
11. To perform and supervise environment monitoring of production unit, Quality Control and allied facilities along with document preparation.
12. To supervise production of Diphtheria, Tetanus and Pertussis vaccine components along with document preparation.
13. To supervise vaccine formulation along with document preparation.
14. Supervision of final containerization, physical inspection, labeling, packing storage of vaccines/sera along with document preparation.
15. To Supervise and perform IPQC/Bulk and final Lot testing of immunobiologicals.
16. To generate protocols for production, Quality Control, containerization, labeling and packing, etc.
17. To prepare documents for submission to regulatory authority for release of vaccines and antisera as per regulatory requirements.
18. To witness and ensure that production process, environment monitoring, preventive maintenance, calibration and validation activities are regularly carried out to assure quality through quality management system.
19. To assist Research Officer in teaching and training of M.Sc. (Microbiology) students, certificate course students and trainees from other institutes and undertaking research projects.
20. Correspondence with Q.A, Stores section, Administrative & Hindi section, etc.
21. Reporting to the Officer-in-charge with respect to the ongoing activities.
22. Supervision of various works pertaining to preparation and testing of biological products.
23. Checking of Standard Operating Procedures/ Data Log Book/ Data Record Form, Responsibility for strict compliance of SOPs.
24. Supervision of testing & quality control of biological products for human use, inventory management and manpower management.
25. Responsible for collection, storage and maintenance of retained samples (seed lots, final lots, raw materials, reference standards, diagnostic reagents, etc.) and maintaining their records.
26. To assist Research Officer in maintaining records of the expired or rejected vaccine/ antisera batches and generate documents for their disposal.
27. To ensure procurement of chemicals, raw materials and equipment as per as the approved specifications/ user requirements.
28. Responsible for superveillance and testing of Salmonella and E.coli strains and polio/ influenza/ and other vaccine preventable diseases in coordination with external Government agencies.
29. Responsible for collection/ storage/ safe disposal of samples received for diagnosis of rabies.
30. Responsible for management and maintenance of stocks of indigenous/ imported vaccines for prophylaxis of vaccine preventable diseases.
31. To be part of various institutional committees.

qualificationDescriptions: Matriculation of Equivalent Pass or ITI Pass
